title: Chickpea Tzatziki Salad
description: A refreshing, creamy chickpea salad with yogurt, cucumber, and fresh herbs.

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.originalText: 2 tablespoons olive oil, plus more for serving

ingredients.name: Persian cucumbers
ingredients.originalText: 1 pound Persian cucumbers, halved and cut into 1/2-inch pieces

steps.instruction: To a large bowl, add the yogurt, garlic, olive oil, honey and the juice of half a lemon; whisk to combine. If it seems too thick, loosen it up with a splash of water or more lemon juice. Season well with salt and pepper.
To the yogurt, add the cucumbers, chickpeas, dill, mint and scallions, and squeeze the remaining half lemon over the mixture. Toss to coat. Taste and season well with salt and pepper.
To serve, drizzle with additional olive oil.

title: French Style Peas
description: Classic French braised peas with bacon, pearl onions, and lettuce in a velvety stock.
notes: The beurre manie can be made with ham, duck, or chicken fat instead of butter for variation.

ingredients.name: gem lettuce
ingredients.originalText: 2 heads gem lettuce, washed and finely cut

ingredients.name: butter
ingredients.originalText: Beurre manie (equal soft butter and kneaded flour)

steps.instruction: Render smoked bacon in a hot pan, allow the fat to be drawn out slowly turning down the heat.
Add the diced onion and season with salt and pepper. Add some butter and allow to cook to soften for around 5-10 minutes.
Add the peas and pearl onions and cover with the chicken stock. Bring to a simmer.
Make a beurre manie by kneading equal parts soft butter and flour together. Add to the pot and stir in.
Cover with a cartouche (parchment paper lid) and bake in a 180°C (350°F) oven for 20-25 minutes.

title: One-Pot Lemon Chicken and Rice
description: Crispy chicken thighs nestled on lemony rice with olives and garlic, all in one pot.

ingredients.name: olives
ingredients.originalText: 1 cup pitted Castelvetrano or kalamata olives, smashed and roughly chopped

ingredients.name: shallot
ingredients.originalText: 1 medium shallot or half medium onion, minced

steps.instruction: Heat the oven to 400°F. Pat the chicken thighs dry. Season with 1 tsp each salt, pepper and dried oregano and a pinch of crushed red pepper.
Place a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat, add oil. Add thighs skin side down and cook undisturbed until they self-release, about 5 minutes. Remove and set aside.
Cut 1 lemon into 1/4-inch slices. Add to the pot and cook until caramelized and softened, about 2 minutes. Remove and set aside.
Add the olives, garlic, shallot and 1 tsp each salt, pepper and dried oregano. Cook over medium-low, scraping browned bits, until garlic is fragrant, 2-3 minutes.
Turn heat to high, add the rice and broth, stir to combine and cover until it comes to a boil, about 5 minutes.
Remove from heat, place chicken thighs on top skin side up, cover with lemon slices. Place pot covered into the oven and bake until rice and chicken are fully cooked, 25-30 minutes.
Serve topped with fresh parsley and a squeeze of lemon juice.

title: Caramelized Brussels Sprouts with Lemon
description: Deeply caramelized sprout cores with shatteringly crispy outer leaves, finished with lemon.
notes: Leaves can be separated from cores 1 day ahead. Store separately in the fridge. Leftovers keep 2 days in the fridge.

ingredients.name: brussels sprouts
ingredients.originalText: 2 pounds brussels sprouts, bottoms trimmed only very slightly

ingredients.name: salt and pepper
ingredients.originalText: Salt and freshly ground black pepper

steps.instruction: Heat oven to 350°F. Shave off thin layers from the bottom of each sprout, pulling off darker outer leaves into a bowl, until you have just the tight light-green core. Slice any very large cores in half.

Combine the cores and 2 tbsp oil on a rimmed baking sheet, season with salt and pepper. Toss, arrange cut pieces cut-side down, and roast, shaking once or twice, until a knife slides into cores with just a little resistance, 30-35 minutes.
Remove baking sheet and increase oven to 425°F. Add remaining 2 tbsp oil to the bowl of leaves, season with salt and pepper, toss until coated. Scatter leaves over the baking sheet.
Return to oven and roast until leaves are bright green and crispy and cores are caramelized, 10-15 minutes.
Let cool slightly. Finely grate about half the lemon zest over the sprouts. Just before serving, squeeze half the lemon over the sheet. Season to taste and serve immediately.

title: Orecchiette with Sausage and Broccoli Rabe
description: A classic Southern Italian pasta with crumbled sausage, bitter broccoli rabe, and sharp Pecorino.

ingredients.name: broccoli rabe
ingredients.originalText: 1 bunch broccoli rabe, trimmed and cut into 2-inch pieces

steps.instruction: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add broccoli rabe and blanch for 2 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside. Add orecchiette to the same water and cook until al dente.
Meanwhile, heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add sausage and break into small pieces. Cook until browned and cooked through, about 6-8 minutes.
Add garlic and red pepper flakes to the sausage and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the blanched broccoli rabe to the skillet and toss to combine. Season with salt.
Drain pasta, reserving 1/2 cup pasta water. Add pasta to the skillet, toss everything together, adding pasta water as needed to create a light sauce. Finish with grated Pecorino Romano.

title: Shrimp Ceviche
description: Bright and zesty shrimp ceviche with fresh vegetables, lime, and a touch of heat.
notes: Let the shrimp marinate in citrus for at least 30 minutes (up to 2 hours) for best flavor. Add avocado just before serving.

steps.instruction: Bring a pot of salted water to a boil. Add shrimp and cook until just pink, about 2-3 minutes. Drain and immediately transfer to an ice bath. Once cool, chop into bite-size pieces.
Combine chopped shrimp with lime juice and lemon juice in a bowl. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Add the tomato, red onion, jalapeno, cilantro, cucumber, and avocado. Toss gently to combine.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve chilled with tortilla chips.

title: Beef Bourguignon
description: The classic French beef stew braised in red wine with mushrooms, pearl onions, and bacon.
notes: Even better the next day. Serve with crusty bread, egg noodles, or mashed potatoes.

ingredients.name: beef chuck
ingredients.originalText: 3 lbs beef chuck, cut into 2-inch cubes

ingredients.name: dry red wine
ingredients.originalText: 1 bottle (750ml) dry red wine (Burgundy or Pinot Noir)

steps.instruction: Cook diced bacon in a Dutch oven until crispy. Remove and set aside. Pat beef dry, season with salt and pepper. Brown in batches in the bacon fat, about 3-4 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
Add carrots and pearl onions to the pot, cook until lightly browned, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook 1 minute more.
Add tomato paste, stir and cook for 1 minute. Sprinkle flour over the vegetables and stir to coat.
Pour in the wine and beef broth, scraping up any browned bits. Add thyme, bay leaves, bacon, and beef back to the pot. Bring to a simmer.
Cover and transfer to a 325°F oven. Braise for 2-3 hours until the beef is fork-tender.
During the last 30 minutes, sauté mushrooms in butter in a separate pan until golden, about 8 minutes. Add to the stew.
Remove bay leaves. Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve garnished with fresh parsley.

title: Zuppa Toscana
description: A hearty Tuscan soup with spicy sausage, potatoes, kale, and a creamy broth.

ingredients.name: Yukon Gold potatoes
ingredients.originalText: 1 lb Yukon Gold potatoes, sliced 1/4-inch thick

ingredients.name: kale
ingredients.originalText: 1 bunch kale, stems removed and roughly chopped

steps.instruction: Brown the sausage in a large pot over medium-high heat, breaking it into crumbles. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, cook bacon until crispy. Remove and set aside, leaving the drippings.
Add onion to the drippings and cook until softened, about 3 minutes. Add garlic and red pepper flakes, cook 1 minute more.
Add chicken broth, water, and potatoes. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer. Cook until potatoes are tender, about 15 minutes.
Add the sausage, bacon, and kale. Simmer until kale is wilted, about 5 minutes.
Stir in heavy cream. Season with salt and pepper. Serve hot.

title: Shrimp Scampi
description: Succulent shrimp in a garlicky lemon-butter wine sauce tossed with linguine.

ingredients.name: shrimp
ingredients.originalText: 1 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined

steps.instruction: Cook linguine in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water, then drain.

While pasta cooks, heat olive oil and 2 tbsp butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Season shrimp with salt and pepper, add to skillet. Cook 1-2 minutes per side until pink. Remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Add remaining 2 tbsp butter and garlic. Cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add white wine, lemon juice, and red pepper flakes. Simmer for 2 minutes, scraping up any browned bits.
Add the cooked pasta and shrimp back to the skillet. Toss to coat, adding pasta water as needed. Finish with parsley. Serve immediately.

title: Aglio e Olio
description: The simplest, most elegant pasta — golden garlic, olive oil, and a touch of heat.
notes: The key is to cook the garlic slowly until golden, never brown. The pasta water creates the silky sauce.

steps.instruction: Cook spaghetti in generously salted boiling water until just shy of al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water, then drain.
While pasta cooks, heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-low heat. Add garlic and red pepper flakes. Cook slowly, stirring occasionally, until garlic is golden (not brown), about 4-5 minutes.
Add the drained pasta to the skillet along with 1/2 cup reserved pasta water. Toss vigorously over medium heat until the pasta is coated and the sauce is emulsified, about 2 minutes.
Remove from heat, toss with parsley. Add more pasta water if needed. Serve with Parmigiano-Reggiano if desired.

title: Carne Guisada
description: A classic Mexican beef stew braised low and slow with tomatoes, chili, and cumin until fork-tender.
notes: Serve with warm flour tortillas, rice, or beans. Even better the next day.

ingredients.name: beef stew meat
ingredients.originalText: 3 lbs cubed beef stew meat or chuck pot roast

ingredients.name: salt and pepper
ingredients.originalText: Salt and black pepper to taste

steps.instruction: Season the cubed beef generously with salt and black pepper.
In a large pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat, brown the beef in batches. Remove and set aside.
In the same pot, sauté the diced onion and green bell pepper until softened, about 5 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the flour and stir to coat the vegetables. Cook for 1-2 minutes.
Add the chili powder, cumin, and oregano. Stir to combine.
Dice the Roma tomatoes and add them to the pot along with the tomato paste. Stir well.
Pour in the beef broth and stir, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom. Return the beef to the pot.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer until the beef is fork-tender and the sauce has thickened, about 1.5 to 2 hours. Stir occasionally.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper. Serve with rice, tortillas, or beans.

title: Tagliolini agli Scampi
description: A simple and elegant Roman pasta from Checco er Carettiere. Fresh egg tagliolini tossed with scampi, cherry tomatoes, garlic, and a touch of chili — ready in minutes.
notes: Stefania's tip: use smaller scampi for pasta — they have more flavor. The key is cooking the scampi only 2 minutes at the end to keep them succulent. The tomatoes should be cooked until almost caramelized for a deeper, sun-dried-tomato-like flavor.

ingredients.name: extra virgin olive oil
ingredients.originalText: Extra virgin olive oil, a generous glug

steps.instruction: Heat a generous amount of extra virgin olive oil in a large pan. Add the garlic clove and a piece of peperoncino. Let them gently sizzle.
While the soffritto heats, peel the scampi: remove the shells and separate the heads. Reserve the heads — do not discard them.
Before the garlic browns, add the halved cherry tomatoes to the pan. Remove the peperoncino (and the garlic if it's getting too dark).
Crush the reserved scampi heads into the pan to release their flavorful juices. Let the sauce simmer while the tomatoes cook down and almost caramelize.
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil and cook the fresh egg tagliolini for 5-7 minutes until al dente.
Remove the scampi heads from the sauce. Add the peeled scampi tails and cook for just 2 minutes — brief cooking keeps them succulent and sweet.
Add the drained tagliolini to the pan with a splash of pasta water. Toss everything together to create a light, creamy sauce.
Plate and finish with a sprinkle of fresh chopped parsley.

title: Steak Tartare
description: Classic French steak tartare with hand-cut beef fillet, capers, cornichons, and egg yolk. A timeless bistro dish — no cooking required.
notes: Always hand-cut the beef with a knife — never use a grinder, which crushes the meat into a puree. Buy from a trusted butcher, not pre-packaged. Use only small capers and drain all vinegar from capers and cornichons. Serve with French fries and a salad for a proper bistro meal.

ingredients.name: beef fillet
ingredients.originalText: 300g beef fillet (150g per person), very lean

ingredients.name: Worcestershire sauce
ingredients.originalText: A touch of Lea & Perrins sauce

steps.instruction: Trim any fat from the beef fillet — you do not want to eat raw fat. Cut the fillet into thin slices, then cut across into small pieces by hand with a sharp knife. Never use a grinder.
Finely chop the cornichons, shallot, and anchovy fillets. Drain the capers well, removing excess vinegar. Everything should be cut quite small.
Place the chopped beef in a bowl. Add the anchovies, cornichons, capers, and shallots. Mix gently.
Add the egg yolks and a touch of Dijon mustard (not English mustard). Add a dash of Worcestershire sauce, salt, and pepper.
Slowly drizzle in a little olive oil while mixing, incorporating the yolk and mustard together with the meat. Don't add too much oil.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper. Serve immediately, shaped on a plate, with French fries and a green salad.
